hero.title: go-i2p
hero.subtitle: I2P networking ecosystem for Go — routers, SAM libraries, tunnels, crypto, and tools.
callout.desc: The go-i2p router is not yet usable. For production I2P today, see i2pd (C++) or Java I2P.
callout.libs: The connection libraries (onramp, goSam, sam3) are stable and work with any I2P router.
desc.goSam: HTTP proxy in ~50 LOC. Simple, configurable.
desc.sam3: Full-featured SAMv3.3. All options, all sharp edges.
desc.go-i2p: Pure-Go I2P router. 97★. Not yet usable.
desc.common: Shared library factored from the router.
desc.i2pkeys: I2P key handling. Used by onramp, goSam, sam3.
desc.go-meta-dialer: One dialer for clearnet, onion, and I2P.
desc.go-meta-listener: Listen and respond on multiple addresses.
desc.gitlab-to-gitea: Migrate repos from GitLab to Gitea.
desc.i2p-control: CLI for I2PControl. Demo of go-i2pcontrol.
desc.util: Utility functions that don't fit elsewhere.
maturity.onramp: mature, implements SAMv3.3, Streaming and Datagrams
maturity.go-sam-go: mature, implements SAMv3.3, Streaming and Datagrams
maturity.go-i2pcontrol: mature but does not provide all possible consts (like ratestats)
section.interface_note: Connection libraries act as drop-in replacements for net.Conn and net.PacketConn in your Go code. When used correctly, they make it easy to adapt Go applications to I2P.
section.quickstart.note: Requires a running I2P router (i2pd or Java I2P). See onramp for full docs.
status.ready.title: ✅ Mature Libraries
status.ready.desc: Connection libraries (onramp, goSam, sam3) are stable and work with any I2P router. The Streaming and Datagram APIs are reliable.
status.dev.title: 🔧 In Development
status.dev.desc: The core router (go-i2p) is operational but not production-safe. I2CP, Streaming, and Datagram client libraries are functional but APIs may change between 0.1.x and 0.2.0.
status.advice.title: 📋 For Developers
status.advice.desc: The API below onramp is unstable. Use high-level libraries to minimize churn. See the router README for detailed status.
